questions.question: List two primary functions of the Android Debug Bridge (adb). (2 marks)
expectedPoints: Installing and uninstalling applications (.apk files) on a connected device/emulator.
Accessing the device's shell to run commands.
Copying files to and from the device.

questions.question: Describe the process of converting human-written source code into a final APK file ready for installation. Name the key tools involved and their specific roles in the process. (5 marks)
expectedPoints: The JDK's `javac` compiles Java/Kotlin source code (.java/.kt) into Java bytecode (.class files).
The Android SDK's `d8` (or `dx`) tool converts the Java bytecode into Dalvik bytecode, resulting in a .dex file.
The `aapt2` tool compiles app resources (XML, images) and packages them, also generating the R.java class for resource IDs.
The build system (Gradle) combines the .dex files, compiled resources, and other assets into an APK file.
The APK file is signed with a debug or release keystore to verify its authenticity before it can be installed.
